Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a task within the OmniPlan AI project management application.

duration.description: Duration of the task in working days.

durationUnit.description: The unit for the task's duration.

start.description: Start date and time of the task.

finish.description: Finish date and time of the task.

percentComplete.description: Percentage of the task that is complete (0-100).

constraintType.description: Type of constraint applied to the task (e.g., 'Start No Earlier Than', 'Must Start On'). Nullable.

constraintDate.description: Date associated with the constraint. Nullable.

zoneId.description: Reference to the Zone entity where the task is spatially located. Nullable. (Relationship: Zone 1:N Task)

customAttributes.description: JSON string containing custom attributes for the task. Nullable.

schedulingConflict.description: Indicates if there is a scheduling conflict for the task.

parentId.description: Reference to the parent task for hierarchical structure.

isCollapsed.description: Whether the summary task is collapsed in the UI.

deadline.description: An optional deadline for the task.

notes.description: A list of notes or activity logs for the task.

additionalNotes.description: Persistent, high-level notes about the task.

Note.title: Note
Note.description: Represents a note or log entry on a task.

Link.title: Link
Link.description: Represents a dependency link between two tasks.

type.description: Type of link (FS, SS, FF, SF).

Zone.title: Zone
Zone.description: Represents a spatial zone defined on the 4D map.

coordinates.description: JSON string containing the coordinates of the polygon defining the zone.

HistoryLog.title: HistoryLog
HistoryLog.description: Represents a log entry for changes made to the application state.

id.description: Unique identifier for the history log entry.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the change occurred.

actionType.description: Type of action that triggered the change (e.g., 'CREATE_TASK', 'UPDATE_TASK').

entityType.description: The entity type that was modified (e.g., 'Task', 'Link').

entityId.description: ID of the specific entity that was modified.

previousState.description: JSON string representing the state of the entity before the change.

newState.description: JSON string representing the state of the entity after the change.

Resource.title: Resource
Resource.description: Represents a resource that can be assigned to tasks.

costPerHour.description: Cost per hour for work resources.

calendarId.description: Reference to the calendar for this resource.

Assignment.title: Assignment
Assignment.description: Links a task to a resource.

Exception.title: Exception
Exception.description: Represents a non-working period in a calendar.

Calendar.title: Calendar
Calendar.description: Defines working and non-working time for scheduling.

View.title: View
View.description: Represents a saved view configuration including columns, grouping, and filters.

params.name: settingId
params.description: The unique identifier for the settings document (e.g., 'app_settings').

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support the OmniPlan AI application, focusing on scalability, security, and maintainability. It leverages structural segregation and denormalization to ensure authorization independence and simplify security rules. The design acknowledges the need for a relational design for tasks, links and zones.

1.  **Authorization Independence:** The structure avoids hierarchical `get()` calls in security rules by denormalizing authorization context where necessary. Since there are no collaborative entities, but rather path-based ownership via the user id, denormalization is simple.
2.  **Structural Segregation:** Each collection houses documents with a homogeneous security posture. Private user data is stored under the `/users/{userId}` path, ensuring only the authenticated user can access their data. Shared project data would be stored at the project level, with its own security rules. Because there is no collaborative context, this is not needed here.
3.  **Access Modeling:** The structure adheres to consistent access patterns. Tasks, Links, Zones, HistoryLogs, Resources, Assignments, and Calendars are nested under `/users/{userId}`, clearly indicating ownership. This path-based ownership simplifies the security rules.
4.  **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structure enables secure `list` operations by ensuring all documents within a collection share the same security requirements. The path-based ownership model allows listing only the documents owned by the authenticated user.
5. **History Logs**: For the prompt's request to persist modifications to the application state, the data structure includes a `HistoryLog` entity. This entity captures changes made to tasks, links, and zones, including the previous and new states of the modified entities. Because this is considered private data, it is nested under the user.

This structure allows for future expansion, such as adding collaborative projects or more complex role-based access control, by introducing new top-level collections with appropriate membership maps and security rules.
